response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with ~18 years of software development experience and genuine developer-tooling credentials (test runners, assertion libraries, CI/CD infrastructure, MCP code generators), but the role is a Level II people-management position and the resume shows no formal people-management or organizational-leadership experience. Technical leadership is implied through founding an open-source platform, workshops, and staff-level titles, yet there is no evidence of conducting performance reviews, managing reports, or leading organizational change, which are core to this role.
strengths: 18+ years of broad software development experience, well beyond the 8-year minimum
Authentic developer-tools background: test frameworks (Trial, Fluent-Asserts), CI/CD setup, device orchestration tooling, and AI/developer tooling at re:cinq
Strong engineering-quality culture fit: TDD, craftsmanship, code katas, teaching workshops and courses
concerns: No formal people-management experience (1:1s, performance management, team development) — a hard requirement of 2+ years
No demonstrated experience leading organizational change or setting team roadmaps at scale
Mobile-platform experience is limited to a brief iOS/Apple TV stint a decade ago, weak for a mobile-ecosystem-focused org

key_factors.factor: Developer tools and testing infrastructure expertise
